# Chapter 31 Progressive Workbook Tasks

The Progressive Workbook feature is currently in development.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/327.2.png)




By default, the Progressive Workbook audit task is not selected. To enable it, click the IGT
Spade, then select Configuration > Modify User Tasks. Move the Progressive Workbook
item from the Groups Available field to the Groups Selected field.

To open the Progressive Workbook:

1\. Click Progressive Workbook in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The
Progressive Workbook task appears.

2\. Click Progressive Workbook. The Progressive Workbook window opens.

The Progressive Workbook window displays active progressives and the estimated progressive
movement based on the meter activity of associated machines. Adjust the following values as
necessary: Total Value, Prior Total, Change, and the Progressive Hit? check box.

If a progressive was hit during the period, the Progressive Hit? check box is selected.




![Figure 31-1 Progressive Workbook](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/327.3.png)




## 31.1 Configuring the Progressive Workbook

The Progressive Workbook feature assists in tracking progressive jackpots by estimating their
size each day based on the meter movement of machines in each pool.

To configure the Progressive Workbook:

Click IGT Spade > Configuration > Configure Progressive Workbook. The
Progressive List window appears and displays active progressives.

### 31.1.1 Adding Progressives

This Progressive Workbook feature is currently in development.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/328.2.png)




#### To add a progressive:

1\. Navigate to the Progressive List window.

2\. Click Add. The Add / Edit Progressive Workbook Item window appears.




![Figure 31-3 Progressive Workbook-Add/Edit Progressive Workbook Item](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/328.3.png)




3\. Enter a description for the progressive in the Description field.

4\. Click the Progressive Type drop-down list, then select one of the following types:

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="328" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 31 Progressive Workbook Tasks" -->

· L, Linked

· M, Manual

· S, Stand Alone

5\. Enter the progressive's bonus ID in the PC-ID-Bonus field.

6\. Enter the progressive's pool ID in the PC-ID-Pool ID field.

7\. Enter the percentage of meter movement that accumulates in the progressive in the
Contribution % field.

8\. Enter the amount to which the progressive returns following a payout in the Reset Value
field.

9\. Click Select Machines. The Select Filter Items window opens.

10\. Select individual EGMs; click Select All to select all EGMs; or use the Denomination,
Section, Bank, and Manufacturer filters to select the participating machines.

11\. Click OK. The machines are added to the Participating Machines pane.

12\. Click Close. The Add / Edit Progressive Workbook Item window closes.


### 31.1.2 Editing Progressives

This Progressive Workbook feature is currently in development.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/329.2.png)




#### To edit a progressive:

1\. Navigate to the Progressive List window.

2\. Select a progressive to edit in the Progressive List window, then click Edit. The Add /
Edit Progressive Workbook Item window appears.

3\. Modify the fields in the Add / Edit Progressive Workbook Item window.

4\. Click OK. The changes save, and the Progressive List window reappears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="329" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 31 Progressive Workbook Tasks" -->


# 31.1.3 Deleting Progressives




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/330.1.png)




This Progressive Workbook feature is currently in development.

To delete a progressive:

1\. Navigate to the Progressive List window.

2\. Select a progressive to delete in the Progressive List window, then click Delete. A
confirmation prompt appears.

3\. Click Yes. The progressive is deleted.

# Appendix A Event Codes and Descriptions

An event is something that happens at the machine, such as a jackpot, a door open, or a game
power on/off. Each event is assigned an event code.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/331.1.png)

<!-- PageHeader="Appendix B Argentina Tax Feature" -->


# Appendix B Argentina Tax Feature

Players in Argentina must pay a tax to the state based on the amount of cash inserted into a
gaming machine and on specific types of jackpot earnings. The ADVANTAGE™M system
supports the following:

· Bill Tax-Calculation and deduction of tax at the time a player inserts cash at the
EGM.

· Win Tax-Calculation and deduction of tax on jackpot earnings large enough to
require a handpay processed through the FJP workstation (on the cash portion only).




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/349.1.png)




This feature includes additional governance features related to collecting the tax,
such as additional reports and changes to existing accounting audit procedures.

Note the following about this feature:

· Bill tax is a calculated percentage of the bill drop (default is 0.95 percent).

· An amount matching the bill tax percentage is deducted (cashed out) from the EGM
upon each bill insertion. For example, with a 0.95 tax percentage, a player inserting
$100.00 receives a $99.05 credit on the EGM.

· Jackpots processed through the FJP workstation (large enough to warrant a handpay)
are subject to a win tax on the cash amount only.

· Beginning with FJP 9.7.4 HF 3, players have three jackpot options: receive the entire
amount in cash, receive the entire amount as an EZ Pay ticket, or divide the jackpot
into cash winnings and an EZ Pay ticket for reinsertion at the gaming machine.

· Deducted tax amounts are rounded up to the nearest cent.

· The bill tax percentage is configurable in the system (from 0.00 to 5.00 percent).

· The win tax percentage is configurable in the system (from 0.00 to 10.00 percent).

· All counters and configuration events associated with the AFT function must be
incorporated.

· The real-time status of the machine pool must be viewable.

· Permission to configure the Options Workbook is required to enable and configure the
Argentina tax feature.

· Additional product dependencies: Advantage Bonusing System (ABS) 9.7.5 and EZ Pay
9.7.5.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="349" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Appendix B Argentina Tax Feature" -->


## B.1 EGM and Hardware Configuration

Configure the following items to support the Argentina tax feature:

· LBE2 and MBE2 are required. (Machines running legacy BE2 hardware are not
supported.)

· SAS 5.10 communication protocol or later

· AFT In-House transfers enabled solely for debits (machines not configured for AFT In-
House transfers remain online but the bill accepter is disabled)

· EGM accounting denomination must be set to $0.01




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/350.1.png)




Refer to the Machine Accounting 9.7.4 HF2 Installation Guide for more
information.


## B.2 Machine Accounting Configuration




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/350.2.png)




Permission to configure the Options Workbook is required to enable and configure
this feature.

To enable the bill tax and/or win tax features in Machine Accounting:


### 1. Navigate to Options > General > Argentina tax deduction.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/350.3.png)




2\. Select Bill tax enabled to enable the bill tax.

3\. Enter the applicable tax amount in the Bill tax rate% field.

Ť

The Billtaxtrasactionexpiredays (MA Accounting db/dbo.config) range is 1 to
1,000 days (default = 30 days; 0 = feature disabled). All transactions exceeding the
number of days indicated in the Billtaxtrasactionexpiredays setting are
purged from the Billtransaction table.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="350" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Appendix B Argentina Tax Feature" -->

4\. Select Win tax enabled to enable the win tax.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/351.1.png)




Ť
When enabled, the Argentina win tax percentage is only deducted from the cash
portion of a handpay jackpot processed at the FJP Station. The win tax is not
deducted from any portion of the jackpot returned to the player as an EZ Pay
ticket.

5\. Enter the applicable tax amount in the Win tax rate% field.

6\. Click OK. The settings update.


## B.3 FJP Configuration

To enable the ability to divide a single handpay into a cash and EZ Pay ticket:
1\. Navigate to Options > General > FJP > FJP Configuration > Limit Settings.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/351.2.png)




2\. Select FJP Handpay Enable Split.

3\. Click OK.
i




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/351.3.png)




The Argentina Win Tax is deducted from the cash portion of the handpay only.
No tax is deducted from the EZ Pay ticket portion of the handpay.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="351" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Appendix B Argentina Tax Feature" -->


## B.4 Machine Accounting Auditing

Audit tasks for the Argentina Bill TaxOut process include:

· Importing the Bill TaxOut information

· Auditing the Bill TaxOut meters




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/352.1.png)




## B.5 Reports

The following reports support the Argentina tax feature:

· Argentina EGM Event-Configuration events associated with the AFT function (enable
and disabled status) and the AFT transfer event.

· Argentina Master Machine-Columns for User ID Unit, the EGMs' SAS version, AFT
supported/enabled, and when bill acceptance is blocked.

· Argentina Bill Tax Calculation-Bill tax information for a single day or for a given
range of days.

· Argentina Bill and Win Tax Calculation-Total bill tax and win tax information for a
single day or for a given range of days.

· Argentina Win Tax Calculation-Win tax information for a single day or for a given
range of days.

Changes to the following existing reports include:

· Meter Reading on Demand-Argentina Tax Out field.

· Machine Event and Meters-Tax Out field.

· Meter Test-Tax Out field.

· Period Log-Records the bill and win tax configurations (MA > Options) as well as
any updates made on the Argentina Bill TaxOut user task.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/352.2.png)




Refer to the Machine Accounting 9.7.4 HF2 Report Glossary (IGT p/n 69-200560-
xx) for more information.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="352" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Appendix B Argentina Tax Feature" -->
